commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional `accountId` metadata to agent hook contexts and forwards it through shared hook context building, `before_agent_reply`, and `resolve_exec_env` dispatch.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +29. Total +52 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature/API PR. Source inspection confirms current main lacks `accountId` on `PluginHookAgentContext` and in the two touched dispatch contexts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer API approval and contributor real behavior proof are needed before merge; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): surface accountId on agent hook context
target.body:
## What

Adds an optional `accountId` field to `PluginHookAgentContext` and propagates it through `before_agent_reply` and `resolve_exec_env` hook dispatch sites, plus the shared `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` builder used by every agent run / cli-runner / embedded-runner path.

## Why

Multi-account channel deployments (multiple Feishu apps / multiple Slack workspaces / etc. served by one OpenClaw instance) already track an `accountId` internally for routing and auth. Today plugins receiving agent hooks have no canonical way to recover this:

- `sessionKey` encodes the account-id only on some surfaces. Notably absent in:
  - feishu group conversations (`agent:<id>:feishu:group:<chatId>` — no accountId segment)
  - cron-triggered runs (`cron:<job_id>` — completely independent shape)
- Plugins end up parsing internal sessionKey shapes or falling back to top-level defaults, neither of which is reliable.

This makes account-aware plugin behavior (per-account metrics, audit attribution, exec-subprocess identity preparation, routing decisions) brittle or unimplementable today.

## Change

- `PluginHookAgentContext` — new optional `accountId?: string` field.
- `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` — accepts both `accountId` (new canonical name) and `agentAccountId` (existing internal alias used by `RunEmbeddedAgentParams` and tool-runtime context). All existing `...buildAgentHookContextChannelFields(params)` spread call sites in embedded-runner / cli-runner / cron paths forward the field for free via the `agentAccountId` alias — no rename churn.
- `runBeforeAgentReply` ctx (get-reply.ts) — sources from `sessionCtx.AccountId ?? ctx.AccountId`, mirroring existing reads in the same file.
- `runResolveExecEnv` ctx (bash-tools.exec.ts) — sources from `defaults?.accountId ?? context?.hookContext?.accountId`, mirroring the existing `defaults?.accountId` use at the notify call site above.

## Compatibility

Fully additive. Plugins that don't read `accountId` are unaffected. Builder accepts both the new `accountId` field and the legacy `agentAccountId` alias, so all existing internal call sites keep working without code changes.

## Verification

- Added unit tests in `src/plugins/hook-agent-context.test.ts`: accountId surfacing / agentAccountId alias / both-supplied precedence — 9/9 passed.
- Sanity-ran adjacent hook callsite tests:
  `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.before-agent-reply.test.ts` 2/2,
  `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.resolve-env-hook.test.ts` 8/8,
  `src/agents/cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts` 9/9.

Refs: #87668
target.author: yukunlabs
